title: cleistopholis glauca
intro: Cleistopholis glauca, also known as the West African claw flower, is a plant species native to tropical regions of West Africa. Its medicinal properties have been utilized for centuries, primarily due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Cleistopholis glauca include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic for wound care. Cleistopholis glauca can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of a decoction or infusion, often used in traditional African medicine to alleviate various ailments.
